response.justification: The candidate's profile is fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. C++ experience is negligible — approximately six months at National Instruments and a university thesis project — against a mandatory 5–10 years on Windows. The career is built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and JavaScript. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle work, no Windows platform development, and no fixed-income or capital markets domain knowledge applied in a C++ context. The brief RIDE Capital engagement involved Node.js and GraphQL, not financial-product engineering.
strengths: Broad polyglot background with exposure to low-latency, high-throughput systems (DLang async I/O at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests/second)
Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps practice (GitLab, Docker, Artifactory, trunk-based development at RIDE Capital)
Some financial services exposure through RIDE Capital engagement, showing familiarity with a regulated-industry environment
concerns: C++ experience is effectively absent — roughly six months at National Instruments plus a university thesis — against a mandatory 5–10 year hands-on requirement on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience mentioned anywhere; database work is limited to MongoDB and generic SQL references
Zero fixed-income, fixed-income derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge applied in any engineering context
